message:
feat(core,abi): port /etc/aliases parser to core::aliases (bd-al-epic)

Lifts the parse_aliases_line byte-slice parser (~50 lines including
two custom trim helpers) out of unistd_abi.rs into a pure-safe
frankenlibc_core::aliases module.

New core surface:
  pub struct AliasEntry { name: Vec<u8>, members: Vec<Vec<u8>> }
  pub fn parse_aliases_line(line: &[u8]) -> Option<AliasEntry>
  pub fn lookup_alias_by_name(content, name) -> Option<AliasEntry>
  pub fn parse_all_aliases(content) -> Vec<AliasEntry>

The parser:
  - strips inline `#` comments
  - tolerates trailing whitespace, \n, \r
  - bidirectionally trims the name and rejects empty
  - trims each comma-separated member, filters out empties

lookup_alias_by_name folds names case-insensitively (per RFC 5321
mailbox local-part convention used by every MTA in practice).

abi changes (4 call sites):
  - alias_iter_next, getaliasbyname, getaliasbyname_r, getaliasent_r
    all delegate to core::aliases::parse_aliases_line and access
    entry.name / entry.members on the owned struct.

The C-ABI struct aliasent marshalling — caller-buffer NUL packing,
member pointer-array layout, the alias_local trailing field — stays
at the boundary, where it has to be.

Verified:
  - 18 new core::aliases unit tests pass
  - 2/2 alias_* unistd_abi integration tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass

Closes bd-al-epic.
